preFix.title: Bird's nest baklava
preFix.subtitle: Filo rolls coiled into nests with pistachio filling
preFix.excerpt: Bird's nest baklava (osmaliye) are individual filo rolls shaped into round nests, filled with chopped pistachios, baked, and soaked in orange blossom syrup. Each nest is formed by rolling a strip of buttered filo around a thin dowel, then sliding it off and coiling it into a round. They look more elaborate than they are.
preFix.sourceNotes: Bird's nest baklava is a Syrian and Lebanese pastry tradition, distinct from the layered Turkish baklava in its rolled and coiled form. The shape is associated with Levantine and Egyptian confectionery traditions and appears in Middle Eastern pastry shops throughout the region. The use of orange blossom water in the syrup is characteristic of Levantine confectionery, where it is preferred over the rosewater used in some other traditions. The pistachio filling reflects the Syrian origin, where pistachios are grown and prized.

content.text: The technique for forming nests is to roll a strip of buttered filo around a thin wooden spoon handle or dowel, slide it off the handle, then coil the ruffled cylinder into a round nest shape. The filo crimps and ruffles as it compresses, which creates the decorative texture. Work quickly and keep the unworked filo covered with a damp cloth. Once formed, the nests are filled with a spoonful of pistachio mixture pressed into the centre.

Combine 250 g caster sugar, 150 ml water, and the juice of half a lemon in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes until slightly thickened. Remove from the heat, stir in 1 tablespoon of orange blossom water, and leave to cool completely. The syrup must be cold when it goes over the hot pastry.

Heat the oven to 170 degrees C fan. Butter a large baking tray. Mix the finely chopped pistachios with 2 tablespoons of caster sugar and set aside. Cut the filo into strips about 35 cm long and 12 cm wide. Brush each strip generously with melted butter. Place a thin wooden spoon handle at one short end and roll the strip loosely around it, ruffling slightly as you go. Slide off the handle. Coil into a round nest shape and press the end to seal. Place on the tray. Repeat with remaining filo. Press a heaped teaspoon of pistachio mixture into the centre of each nest. Brush the outside of the nests with more melted butter.

Bake for 25 to 32 minutes until the nests are deep gold all the way through. Remove from the oven and immediately pour the cold syrup slowly over the hot nests, working from a spoon to distribute it evenly. Leave to absorb at room temperature for at least 2 hours. Scatter extra chopped pistachios over the top before serving.

items.solution: Place the nests close together on the tray so they support each other, or secure the outer end with a small dot of water before baking. The coil tightens as the butter cooks.

items.problem: The filo is not deep gold but the outside layer looks dark
items.solution: Filo browns from the outside in. Check the underside by lifting gently with a palette knife: if the base is pale, return to the oven for a further 5 minutes.
